rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re Amended Petition Of Ernest Lentfer To Perpetuate Testimony Of Minor Lentfer By Videotaped Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, August 4, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00a.m., Line 1. Ernest Lentfer¿s amended petition to perpetuate testimony of Minor Lentfer by videotaped deposition is denied. The petition fails to set forth facts why any of the parties expected to be named as defendants in the anticipated survival and wrongful death action were not named as defendants in Minor Lentfer¿s personal injury lawsuit. Nor does the petition state as to any of the parties expected to be named as defendants in the anticipated survival and wrongful death lawsuit when it was determined that Minor Lentfer had relevant testimony to provide as to those parties or whether any of those parties were mentioned in discovery responses served by Minor Lentfer in his personal injury lawsuit so that the defendants who were named in that lawsuit could make appropriate Proposition 51 inquiry about them. Absent unusual circumstances, it is a misuse of judicial resources and contrary to the purposes underlying Proposition 51 to permit trial preservation testimony of a person after his own asbestos personal injury lawsuit has been resolved. No such unusual circumstances have been shown here. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel The Annual Update To Responses To General Order 129 Standard Plaintiffs' Interrogatories From Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, August 4, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00a.m., Line 2. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel the annual update to responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiff¿s interrogatories from defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc. is granted. Defendant must file and serve its annual update within 30 days. As long as the annual update provision of General Order 129 remains in effect and has not been invalidated, all parties must comply with its terms. Defendant has not shown that it is impossible for it to comply with the annual update requirement. Because defendant¿s due process argument does not lack substantial justification and plaintiffs¿ counsel was not very responsive in meet and confer efforts, plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. = (220/HEK)
